title:
Commentary on Membership
(Members data presented are as of December 31, 2026)
content:
The credit union’s membership count remained stable year over year and quarter over quarter at the end of Q1’26.

Members per full-time employees showed a slight decline in Q1’26 relative to YE'25. This indicates that staffing levels are slightly ahead of current service demand, though not by enough to warrant concern about misalignment or excess capacity.

Shares per member rose during the period under review. Existing members hold larger average deposit balances now than before, which strengthens the funding base even if total membership remains unchanged. The rise in shares-per-member complements the flat membership count reported earlier and supports a more resilient deposit profile across the portfolio.

The credit union’s stable headcount combined with rising deposits per member reflects an efficient balance between service capacity and capital depth during this period. Continued focus on engagement and retention will support sustained growth in both membership quality and funding stability.
